fix-A--fd-installation.description: Verify fd is available system-wide and AGENTS.md references it correctly
fix-A--fd-installation.bisect_script: which fd && fd --version && fd --list-details && echo '---' && grep -c 'fd' AGENTS.md
fix-A--fd-installation.bisect_expected_output: fd path and version printed; AGENTS.md grep count > 0
smoke_test_steps: Run: fd -e ts -e tsx . packages/opencode/src | wc -l  (should find TypeScript files)
Run: fd -e json package.json packages | wc -l  (should find package.json files)
Run: 'grep fd AGENTS.md' to confirm fd is referenced in the agent instructions
Verify: 'fd --unrestricted' respects .gitignore by default
rollback_verification: grep fd AGENTS.md → verify reference removed or gated with check
ci_gate_verification: The fd check should be part of CI verification in the typecheck workflow: add a step that runs 'which fd' to confirm CI agents have it
PR check: verify AGENTS.md contains fd reference in the CLI tools section

fix-B--build-tool-consolidation.description: Verify typecheck still passes for all 19 migrated packages after build tool changes (tsgo → consolidated toolchain)
smoke_test_steps: 1. Run: 'bun turbo typecheck' from repo root — must exit 0
2. Run individual package typechecks: 'bun --cwd packages/opencode typecheck' (use new consolidated command)
3. Verify build outputs: 'bun --cwd packages/opencode build' produces dist/
4. Verify CI workflow typecheck.yml still uses correct command (check .github/workflows/typecheck.yml)
5. Check all 19 package.json files have consistent typecheck script definitions
6. Verify husky pre-commit hook still works if typecheck is wired there
rollback_verification: Verify 'bun turbo typecheck' still passes with reverted config
Verify CI workflow typecheck.yml still passes on reverted config
ci_gate_verification: Push a branch with the changes and verify the typecheck CI workflow passes on the PR
Verify no package is accidentally excluded from the typecheck matrix
Check build artifacts exist for packages that depend on ^build (opencode, app, ui tests)

fix-C--test-coverage.description: Verify coverage reports are generated, CI gate works, threshold enforcement
fix-C--test-coverage.bisect_script: bun --cwd packages/opencode test --coverage 2>&1 | tail -20; echo 'EXIT_CODE:'$?; ls -la packages/opencode/coverage/ 2>/dev/null || echo 'NO COVERAGE DIR'
fix-C--test-coverage.bisect_expected_output: coverage directory exists with lcov.info or similar; EXIT_CODE:0 (or 1 if below threshold)
smoke_test_steps: 1. Run: 'bun --cwd packages/opencode test --coverage' — verify coverage report generated
2. Check artifacts: 'ls packages/opencode/coverage/' — must contain lcov.info or HTML report
3. Verify threshold: add a deliberate uncovered line, run coverage again — must fail if below threshold
4. Check CI: verify test.yml or a new coverage workflow uploads coverage artifacts
5. Check coverage gate: verify PRs below threshold show failing check
6. Check report format: junit.xml + coverage report both available in CI artifacts
rollback_verification: Verify tests still pass without coverage overhead
Verify CI workflow doesn't break when coverage step is removed
ci_gate_verification: In CI: verify 'bun turbo test:ci' (or new coverage variant) produces coverage artifacts
Check junit report is uploaded: packages/*/.artifacts/unit/junit.xml
Verify coverage report is also uploaded as a CI artifact
If using a coverage threshold: push a branch that falls below it and verify the CI check fails
Push a branch that meets the threshold and verify the CI check passes

fix-D--dead-test-wiring.description: Verify all 4 packages' tests actually run in CI. Currently opencode/app/ui have tests but sdk has none.
fix-D--dead-test-wiring.bisect_script: bun turbo test:ci 2>&1; echo 'EXIT_CODE:'$?; echo '---JUNIT---'; find packages -name 'junit.xml' -path '*/.artifacts/*' 2>/dev/null | sort
fix-D--dead-test-wiring.bisect_expected_output: EXIT_CODE:0; 3 or 4 junit.xml files found (opencode, app, ui; sdk is either added or explicitly excluded)
smoke_test_steps: 1. Identify the 4 packages with turbo.json test tasks: opencode, @opencode-ai/app, @opencode-ai/ui, @opencode-ai/sdk
2. For @opencode-ai/sdk: verify if test script exists in packages/sdk/js/package.json (currently: NO)
3. Either add a test script to SDK package.json + test files, OR remove sdk from turbo.json test matrix
4. Run: 'bun turbo test:ci' — must exit 0 and NOT skip any package silently
5. Verify each package's .artifacts/unit/junit.xml is generated
6. Check CI test.yml: verify 'bun turbo test:ci' in unit job exercises all 4 packages
7. Verify PG, DuckDB, and E2E test jobs in CI still work (they use package-specific commands, not turbo)
8. Run individual package tests: 'bun --cwd packages/opencode test', 'bun --cwd packages/app test:unit', etc.
rollback_verification: If sdk test script was added: remove it, verify turbo.json reverts to exclude sdk
If sdk was excluded from turbo.json: add it back, verify it fails cleanly (no silent skip)
Verify CI test.yml still runs all existing test matrix jobs
ci_gate_verification: Push a branch with fixed turbo.json + sdk test infrastructure
Verify CI test.yml unit job runs and junit reports show all 4 packages
Verify no package test is silently skipped (check CI logs for 'no task' or 'cache miss')
Verify the Publish Unit Reports step finds junit.xml from all packages

fix-E--adr-template.description: Verify ADR template file structure and AGENTS.md cross-reference
fix-E--adr-template.bisect_script: test -f docs/adr/template.md && echo 'template exists' || echo 'template MISSING'; echo '---'; grep -c 'adr' AGENTS.md 2>/dev/null; echo '---'; head -20 docs/adr/template.md 2>/dev/null || echo 'NO TEMPLATE'
fix-E--adr-template.bisect_expected_output: template exists; AGENTS.md contains adr reference; template has expected sections
smoke_test_steps: 1. Verify: 'ls docs/adr/template.md' — file exists
2. Check template structure: must include sections like Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences
3. Verify: 'grep adr AGENTS.md' — AGENTS.md cross-references the ADR process
4. Create a test ADR using the template: copy template → fill in fields → verify markdown lint passes
5. Verify README.md or CONTRIBUTING.md also references ADR process if applicable
ci_gate_verification: Add a CI check (e.g., in pr-standards.yml or a new workflow) that verifies template.md exists and has required sections
Consider a markdown lint step for docs/adr/*.md files
Verify PR template includes ADR prompt for architectural decisions

fix-F--script-centralization.description: Verify justfile recipes work, migrated scripts still function, no circular imports
fix-F--script-centralization.bisect_script: test -f justfile && echo 'justfile exists' || echo 'justfile MISSING'; echo '---'; just --list 2>&1; echo 'EXIT_CODE:'$?
fix-F--script-centralization.bisect_expected_output: justfile exists; just --list shows recipes; EXIT_CODE:0
smoke_test_steps: 1. Verify: 'test -f justfile' — justfile exists at repo root
2. Run: 'just --list' — all expected recipes appear (typecheck, test, build, lint, etc.)
3. Run: 'just typecheck' — must exit 0 (equivalent to 'bun turbo typecheck')
4. Run: 'just test' — must exit 0 (equivalent to 'bun turbo test:ci')
5. Run: 'just build' — must produce all build artifacts
6. For each migrated script: verify the original script path works AND the just recipe works identically
7. Check for circular imports: 'just typecheck' must not hang or recurse
8. Verify existing scripts in packages/*/script/ still work independently (e.g., 'bun --cwd packages/opencode build' still works)
9. Check husky hooks: if pre-commit used direct script paths, verify they work through just
rollback_verification: Run original commands directly (bun turbo typecheck, bun --cwd packages/opencode test, etc.)
Verify all original paths still work
Verify no CI workflow was modified to depend on just (unless intentional)
ci_gate_verification: Verify CI workflows use just recipes consistently (or a mix of just + direct commands if intentional)
Push a branch and verify CI still runs all jobs with the new just-based commands
If CI uses just: verify just is installed in the CI runner (add to setup-bun action or a separate step)
Check generate.yml: verify './script/generate.ts' either works via just or is kept as direct call

cross_cutting_validations.description: Validations that span multiple fixes and must pass for the overall plan

full_ci_simulation.command: echo 'Simulating full CI pipeline locally...'; bun typecheck && echo 'typecheck OK' && bun --cwd packages/opencode test --timeout 30000 2>&1 | tail -5 && echo 'opencode test OK' && bun --cwd packages/app test:unit 2>&1 | tail -5 && echo 'app test OK' && bun --cwd packages/ui test 2>&1 | tail -5 && echo 'ui test OK'

parallel_lane_safety.description: Verify changes don't conflict across lanes (fix-A through fix-F are independent)
checks: fix-A (fd install) and fix-D (test wiring) touch AGENTS.md — use fragment protocol if concurrent
fix-B (typecheck) and fix-C (coverage) may both modify package.json scripts — use fragment protocol
fix-F (justfile) may create scripts/ directory — ensure it doesn't collide with other lane outputs
